region.name: Delhi
quickFacts: Hindi and English are widely spoken

city.name: New Delhi
quickFacts: Connaught Place is the commercial hub

alerts.title: Odd-Even Vehicle Rule
alerts.description: Vehicles with odd-numbered license plates are banned on odd-numbered dates, even-numbered plates on even dates. Exemptions include two-wheelers, commercial vehicles, and emergency vehicles. Violators face a fine of INR 4,000 and 3 penalty points.

alerts.title: Single-Use Plastic Ban
alerts.description: All single-use plastic items including bags, straws, bottles, and packaging are banned in Delhi. Establishments found violating the ban face fines up to INR 25,000 and possible closure.

alerts.title: Smoking Ban in Public Places
alerts.description: Smoking is prohibited in public spaces including parks, markets, public transport, and within 100 meters of schools and hospitals. Violators face a fine of INR 200 for first offense and INR 500 for subsequent offenses.

alerts.title: Firecracker Ban During Diwali
alerts.description: Firecrackers and fireworks are banned in Delhi from 15 days before Diwali to 2 months after. Possession or bursting crackers can result in INR 5,000 fine and up to 6 months imprisonment.
alerts.source: Delhi Pollution Control Committee & Supreme Court

alerts.title: Vehicle Pollution Norms (BS-VI)
alerts.description: All vehicles must comply with BS-VI emission standards. Vehicles not meeting these norms can be impounded. Get your vehicle emissions tested and maintain a valid pollution certificate.

alerts.title: No Honking Rule in Silence Zones
alerts.description: Honking is strictly prohibited near hospitals, courts, and educational institutions during specified hours. Violators face a fine of INR 1,000. Use horn only when absolutely necessary for safety.

alerts.title: Helmet and Seatbelt Mandatory
alerts.description: Wearing a helmet while riding two-wheelers and seatbelts while driving four-wheelers is mandatory. Non-compliance results in a fine of INR 500 and 2-3 penalty points on your license.

alerts.title: Construction Dust Control Norms
alerts.description: All construction sites must implement dust control measures including water sprinkling, dust screens, and proper waste management. Non-compliance can result in site closure and penalties up to INR 50,000.

alerts.title: Water Conservation and Rationing
alerts.description: During summer months, water supply is rationed. Restrict water usage for non-essential purposes. Violators of water conservation guidelines may face penalties and supply restrictions.

alerts.title: Monument Etiquette and Respect
alerts.description: When visiting monuments like Red Fort, India Gate, and Qutb Minar, maintain respectful behavior. Remove footwear in certain areas, don't touch carvings or inscriptions, and follow posted guidelines. Photography restrictions may apply in some zones.

alerts.title: Religious Site Customs and Protocols
alerts.description: Respect customs at temples, mosques, gurudwaras, and churches. Remove footwear before entering temples and gurudwaras, cover head and shoulders at mosques, and maintain silence in prayer areas. Ask permission before photographing.

alerts.title: Festival Celebration Norms - Holi
alerts.description: During Holi, use organic, herbal colors instead of synthetic ones. Be mindful of water usage in color play, and respect those who don't wish to celebrate. Avoid playing pranks that could offend or cause harm.

alerts.title: Market Haggling and Bargaining Norms
alerts.description: In traditional markets like Chandni Chowk and Sadar Bazaar, haggling is expected and accepted. Prices can often be negotiated, especially for larger purchases. Be respectful and polite; aggressive bargaining is considered disrespectful.

alerts.title: Traditional Cuisine Etiquette
alerts.description: When dining at traditional Delhi restaurants or street food stalls, eating with your right hand is considered respectful. For meals served on thali, it's customary to finish what you take. Tipping is optional but appreciated.

alerts.title: Dress Code Sensitivity in Public
alerts.description: While Delhi is cosmopolitan, modest dress is appreciated, especially in religious areas and conservative neighborhoods. Avoid revealing clothing near temples, mosques, and gurudwaras. Beach-like attire is inappropriate in public transport and markets.

alerts.title: Language and Greetings
alerts.description: Learning basic Hindi greetings like 'Namaste', 'Shukriya', and 'Kripaya' is appreciated. While English is widely understood, speaking Hindi shows respect for local culture. Many locals appreciate efforts to communicate in their language.

alerts.title: Auto-Rickshaw and Taxi Negotiation
alerts.description: Always agree on the fare before boarding autos that don't use meters, or ensure the meter is reset. Use authorized taxis and ride-sharing apps for safer travel. Keep small change ready and negotiate politely if the quoted price seems high.

alerts.title: Air Quality Precautions and Health
alerts.description: Delhi experiences severe air pollution during winter months (Oct-Feb). Wear N95 or N99 masks when AQI exceeds 300. Avoid outdoor exercise, stay indoors, use air purifiers, and keep medications handy if you have respiratory issues.
alerts.source: Delhi Pollution Control Committee & Health Dept

alerts.title: Water Safety and Drinking Water
alerts.description: Don't drink tap water directly; use filtered or bottled water. Avoid ice in drinks at street food stalls unless sure of water source. Use hand sanitizer after public transport. Tap water can be used for bathing after boiling or filtering.

alerts.title: Traffic and Road Behavior
alerts.description: Follow traffic signals strictly. Delhi drivers are aggressive; maintain distance from vehicles, avoid sudden lane changes, and never use mobile phones while driving. Use designated crossings for pedestrians and be alert for two-wheelers.

alerts.title: Metro Etiquette and Conduct
alerts.description: During peak hours, be prepared for crowded coaches. Give priority seating to elderly, pregnant women, and disabled persons. Avoid eating and littering. Don't block doors, keep valuables safe, and use headphones for music. Queue properly at stations.

alerts.title: Street Safety and Awareness
alerts.description: Stay aware of your surroundings, especially in crowded areas and at night. Keep valuables hidden, avoid walking alone late at night, and use well-lit routes. Be cautious of pickpockets in markets and public transport. Travel in groups when possible.
